subject: Social Science - History
chapter_name: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the traditional education system in India before British rule
Explain the British view of Indian education
Understand the debate between Anglicists and Orientalists
Identify the role of Macaulay’s Minute in shaping colonial education policy
Explain the importance of Wood’s Despatch of 1854
Recognize the role of Indian reformers in promoting modern education
Understand the impact of colonial education on Indian society and nationalism

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Traditional Education in India
student_activities: Discuss how students might have learned in ancient India

lesson_plan.title: British Views on Indian Education
student_activities: Identify differences between traditional and British education
Discuss reasons for introducing English education

lesson_plan.title: Debate Between Anglicists and Orientalists
student_activities: Create a comparison list of Anglicists and Orientalists
Participate in a short classroom debate

lesson_plan.title: Macaulay’s Minute (1835)
topics_covered: English as the medium of instruction
teaching_strategy: Reading key statements from the textbook
student_activities: Write a short note on Macaulay’s ideas
Discuss advantages and disadvantages of English education

lesson_plan.title: Wood’s Despatch and Education Reforms
student_activities: Locate Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras on the map

lesson_plan.title: Role of Indian Reformers
student_activities: Write a short paragraph about Raja Rammohan Roy
Discuss how education can bring social reform

lesson_plan.title: Impact of Colonial Education and Revision
student_activities: List major impacts of colonial education

teacher_tip: Encourage discussions and role-play activities such as debates between Anglicists and Orientalists to help students understand the historical arguments about education policies during British rule.
